title: Jet Ski in Santorini: Costs, Seasons, and Tips for 2026
html:
Exploring Santorini by jet ski offers an exhilarating way to see the caldera, with rentals from Vlychada Marina starting at €120 per hour. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators to make your booking seamless.

How much does it cost to rent a jet ski in Santorini?

Jet ski hire in Santorini typically costs between €120 and €250 per hour, depending on the model and season. Half-day rentals (4 hours) range from €350 to €600, and guided tours to the volcanic islands start at €150 per person.

  • Shared guided tour (1.5 hours): €80 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day: €300 from EUR
  • Catamaran full-day (shared): €150 per person
  • Full-day crewed yacht: €1,200 from EUR

For the best rates, browse boats for your dates directly on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to ride a jet ski in Santorini?

No specific licence is required for jet ski rental in Greece, but you must be at least 18 years old and sign a liability waiver. Operators provide a brief safety briefing and may ask for proof of experience. The local principle is that you need a coastal licence for larger boats, but for jet skis, the rental operator confirms exact requirements.

When renting a jet ski in Santorini, always check the weather forecast. The Meltemi wind, which blows from July to August, can create choppy conditions on the caldera, making morning departures smoother.

Where to set off in Santorini

Jet ski rentals operate from several marinas and beaches around the island. Vlychada Marina on the south coast is a popular departure point, offering sheltered waters and direct access to the caldera. Other key launch spots include:

  • Ammoudi Bay, a small harbour below Oia, ideal for sunset rides
  • Perivolos Beach, on the south-east coast, where several rental kiosks operate
  • Athinios Port, the main ferry port, with jet ski hire for experienced riders
  • Palea Kameni, a volcanic island within the caldera, often a stop on guided tours
  • Thirassia Island, reachable via a 2 nautical mile ride from Ammoudi Bay

Which boat to rent

If you prefer a boat over a jet ski, consider these options from BoatHire24:

  1. Motor yacht, Best for luxury day trips, up to 8 guests, from €600 half-day. A popular choice is this motor yacht which offers comfort and speed.
  2. Catamaran, Ideal for groups, stable cruising, up to 12 guests, from €900 full-day
  3. RIB boat, Agile and fast, up to 6 guests, from €250 half-day
  4. Sailing yacht, For sunset excursions, up to 6 guests, from €400 half-day
  5. Jet ski, Solo or tandem, from €120 per hour

When is the best season to hire a jet ski in Santorini?

The high season runs from June to September, with peak conditions in July and August. The Meltemi wind often picks up in the afternoon, so plan morning trips. Water temperatures are warmest in August, reaching 25°C. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Santorini or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: €120, €600 per rental
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 1 hour to full day
  • Capacity: 1, 2 persons per jet ski
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about jet ski rental in Santorini

Can I ride a jet ski without prior experience in Santorini?

Yes, most operators offer a 10-minute instructional session before allowing you to ride solo, but you must be over 18 and able to swim.

Is it safe to jet ski in the Santorini caldera?

Yes, the caldera is generally calm in the morning, but you should avoid the area near cruise ship lanes and follow the operator’s route instructions.

What is the cancellation policy for jet ski rentals in Santorini?

Most rentals offer free cancellation up to 24 hours before departure, but confirm directly with the operator as policies vary.

Are there age limits for passengers on a jet ski in Santorini?

Yes, passengers must be at least 6 years old, and children under 12 must wear a life jacket provided by the rental company.

For your next trip in 2026, consider renting a boat instead of a jet ski through browse the listings. If you own a boat in Santorini, you can also list it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Santorini and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
